req.title: Manager updates employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
An authenticated manager shall be able to update an employee through the UI form.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def manager_user():
    """Return a user object with the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "jane.manager"
    user.role = "manager"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_manager_user():
    """Return an authenticated user WITHOUT the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "john.employee"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.role = None
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def existing_employee():
    """Return a representative existing employee record."""
    employee = MagicMock()
    employee.id = 42
    employee.first_name = "Alice"
    employee.last_name = "Smith"
    employee.email = "alice.smith@example.com"
    employee.department = "Engineering"
    employee.job_title = "Software Engineer"
    employee.phone = "555-0100"
    return employee


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """Return a complete, valid form payload for updating an employee."""
    return {
        "first_name": "Alice",
        "last_name": "Smith",
        "email": "alice.smith@example.com",
        "department": "Product",           # changed field
        "job_title": "Senior Engineer",    # changed field
        "phone": "555-0199",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit-log service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def employee_service(existing_employee, audit_service):
    """
    Return a mock employee service pre-wired with helpers so that
    unit tests can swap in real implementations when desired.
    """
    service = MagicMock()
    service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
    service.update.return_value = existing_employee
    service.audit = audit_service
    return service


@pytest.fixture
def ui_form(employee_service, audit_service):
    """
    Minimal stand-in for the UI form controller / view handler.

    In a real project this would be the actual form class or page object.
    Here we keep it as a lightweight callable so tests stay framework-agnostic.
    """
    from employee_ui import EmployeeUpdateForm   # replace with real import
    return EmployeeUpdateForm(
        employee_service=employee_service,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – builds the form without importing a concrete class so that the
# test module itself can be collected even before implementation exists.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_form(employee_service, audit_service):
    """Factory that lazily imports the real form class."""
    try:
        from employee_ui import EmployeeUpdateForm
        return EmployeeUpdateForm(
            employee_service=employee_service,
            audit_service=audit_service,
        )
    except ModuleNotFoundError:
        pytest.skip("employee_ui module not yet implemented")


# ===========================================================================
# 1. ACCESS CONTROL
# ===========================================================================

class TestAccessControl:
    """Only authenticated managers may access and submit the update form."""

    def test_authenticated_manager_can_access_form(
        self, manager_user, existing_employee, employee_service, audit_service
    ):
        """An authenticated manager receives an accessible (HTTP 200) form."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.load(user=manager_user, employee_id=existing_employee.id)
        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_redirected(
        self, unauthenticated_user, existing_employee, employee_service, audit_service
    ):
        """An unauthenticated user is redirected to the login page."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.load(
            user=unauthenticated_user, employee_id=existing_employee.id
        )
        assert response.status_code in (302, 401, 403)

    def test_non_manager_role_is_forbidden(
        self, non_manager_user, existing_employee, employee_service, audit_service
    ):
        """A non-manager authenticated user cannot access the update form."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.load(
            user=non_manager_user, employee_id=existing_employee.id
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_non_manager_submit_is_rejected(
        self,
        non_manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A non-manager POST is rejected; no employee record is updated."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=non_manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403
        employee_service.update.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 2. FORM PRE-POPULATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestFormPrePopulation:
    """The update form must be pre-filled with the employee's current data."""

    def test_form_loads_existing_employee_data(
        self, manager_user, existing_employee, employee_service, audit_service
    ):
        """Verify each field is populated from the employee record."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.load(user=manager_user, employee_id=existing_employee.id)

        assert response.context["form"]["first_name"] == existing_employee.first_name
        assert response.context["form"]["last_name"] == existing_employee.last_name
        assert response.context["form"]["email"] == existing_employee.email
        assert response.context["form"]["department"] == existing_employee.department
        assert response.context["form"]["job_title"] == existing_employee.job_title

    def test_load_unknown_employee_returns_404(
        self, manager_user, employee_service, audit_service
    ):
        """Loading a non-existent employee ID results in a 404 response."""
        employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.load(user=manager_user, employee_id=9999)
        assert response.status_code == 404


# ===========================================================================
# 3. MANDATORY FIELD VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================

MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["first_name", "last_name", "email", "department", "job_title"]


class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """Each mandatory field must be present and non-empty."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_returns_validation_error(
        self,
        missing_field,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Submitting without a mandatory field must fail with HTTP 422."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload}
        del payload[missing_field]

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        assert missing_field in response.context["errors"]

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("empty_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_empty_mandatory_field_returns_validation_error(
        self,
        empty_field,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Submitting a mandatory field as an empty string must fail."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, empty_field: ""}

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        assert empty_field in response.context["errors"]

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("whitespace_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_whitespace_only_mandatory_field_returns_validation_error(
        self,
        whitespace_field,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Whitespace-only values must not satisfy mandatory field validation."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, whitespace_field: "   "}

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        assert whitespace_field in response.context["errors"]

    def test_invalid_email_format_returns_validation_error(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """An email field with an invalid format must be rejected."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "email": "not-an-email"}

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        assert "email" in response.context["errors"]

    def test_no_employee_update_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The employee service must NOT be called when validation fails."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "first_name": ""}

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=payload,
        )

        employee_service.update.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 4. SUCCESSFUL UPDATE
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulUpdate:
    """Happy-path: valid payload submitted by an authenticated manager."""

    def test_successful_update_returns_success_response(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A valid submission must return HTTP 200 (or 302 redirect)."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (200, 302)

    def test_employee_service_called_with_correct_data(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The employee service update method is called with the correct data."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        employee_service.update.assert_called_once_with(
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

    def test_success_message_shown_to_manager(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A confirmation/success message must appear in the response."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        # Allow for either a context message or body content
        has_message = (
            "success" in (response.context or {}).get("messages", [])
            or "successfully updated" in (response.content or "").lower()
        )
        assert has_message

    def test_update_unknown_employee_returns_404(
        self,
        manager_user,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Submitting an update for a non-existent employee ID yields 404."""
        employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=9999,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404


# ===========================================================================
# 5. AUDIT LOGGING
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditLogging:
    """An audit record must be written on every successful update."""

    def test_audit_record_written_on_success(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit service is called exactly once after a successful update."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        audit_service.log.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_manager_identity(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must identify the manager who made the change."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("performed_by") == manager_user.id or \
               kwargs.get("performed_by") == manager_user.username

    def test_audit_record_contains_employee_id(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must reference the updated employee."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("entity_id") == existing_employee.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_action_type(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must declare the action as an UPDATE."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("action", "").upper() == "UPDATE"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must include a timestamp."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        timestamp = kwargs.get("timestamp")
        assert timestamp is not None
        assert isinstance(timestamp, (datetime, str))

    def test_audit_record_contains_changed_fields(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record should capture which fields were changed."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        _, kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args
        changed = kwargs.get("changed_fields") or kwargs.get("details")
        assert changed is not None  # payload / diff must be present

    def test_no_audit_record_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """No audit record is written when the form fails validation."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "last_name": ""}

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=payload,
        )

        audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_audit_record_when_access_denied(
        self,
        non_manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """No audit record is written when the request is forbidden."""
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        form.submit(
            user=non_manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_failure_does_not_silently_corrupt_update(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """
        If the audit service raises, the response must signal an error
        (not silently succeed without the audit trail).
        """
        audit_service.log.side_effect = RuntimeError("Audit store unavailable")

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        # Must NOT return a success status when audit logging fails
        assert response.status_code not in (200, 302)


# ===========================================================================
# 6. CONCURRENCY / IDEMPOTENCY EDGE CASES
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:
    """Additional robustness scenarios."""

    def test_duplicate_submission_does_not_create_duplicate_audit_records(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """
        Submitting the same form twice (e.g. double-click) should produce
        exactly one persisted update + one audit record per submission cycle
        (idempotency handled at service level).
        """
        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)

        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        # Service and audit called once per submit – caller must de-duplicate
        assert employee_service.update.call_count == 2
        assert audit_service.log.call_count == 2

    def test_xss_payload_is_sanitised(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Field values containing script tags must be sanitised or rejected."""
        xss_payload = {
            "first_name": "<script>alert('xss')</script>",
            "last_name": "Smith",
            "email": "alice@example.com",
            "department": "Engineering",
            "job_title": "Engineer",
        }

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=xss_payload,
        )

        if response.status_code in (200, 302):
            # If accepted, the stored value must be sanitised
            stored_call_kwargs = employee_service.update.call_args[1]["data"]
            assert "<script>" not in stored_call_kwargs.get("first_name", "")
        else:
            # Alternatively the form may reject the input outright
            assert response.status_code == 422

    def test_overly_long_field_is_rejected(
        self,
        manager_user,
        existing_employee,
        valid_update_payload,
        employee_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Field values exceeding maximum length constraints must be rejected."""
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "first_name": "A" * 256}

        form = _make_form(employee_service, audit_service)
        response = form.submit(
            user=manager_user,
            employee_id=existing_employee.id,
            data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        assert "first_name" in response.context["errors"]
```

---

### Key design decisions

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Fixtures** | Separated by concern (user roles, employee data, services, form) so each test uses only what it needs. |
| **Parametrize** | All five mandatory fields are driven by `@pytest.mark.parametrize` to avoid copy-paste and ensure full coverage. |
| **Mocks** | `employee_service` and `audit_service` are `MagicMock` objects; tests assert on *calls*, not on persistence, keeping the suite unit-level and fast. |
| **Audit assertions** | Each audit property (actor, entity, action, timestamp, changed fields) gets its own test so failures are pinpointed precisely. |
| **Negative audit tests** | Explicit tests confirm the audit log is **not** written on validation failure or access denial. |
| **Atomic audit/update** | One test verifies that if the audit service throws, the response indicates failure rather than silently swallowing the error. |
| **Edge cases** | XSS sanitisation, field-length limits, and double-submit are included as robustness checks. |

```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
An authenticated manager shall be able to update an employee through the UI form.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def manager_user():
    """Return a user object with the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "jane.manager"
    user.role = "manager"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_manager_user():
    """Return an authenticated user WITHOUT the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "john.employee"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.role = None
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def existing_employee():
    """Return a representative existing employee record."""
    employee = MagicMock()
    employee.id = 42
    employee.first_name = "Alice"
    employee.last_name = "Smith"
    employee.email = "alice.smith@example.com"
    employee.department = "Engineering"
    employee.job_title = "Software Engineer"
    employee.phone = "555-0100"
    return employee


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """Return a complete, valid form payload for updating an employee."""
    return {
        "first_name": "Alice",
        "last_name": "Smith",
        "email": "alice.smith@example.com",
        "department": "Product",           # changed field
        "job_title": "Senior Engineer",    # changed field
        "phone": "555-0199",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit-log service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def employee_service(existing_employee, audit_service):
    """
    Return a mock employee service pre-wired with helpers so that
    unit tests can swap in real implementations when desired.
    """
    service = MagicMock()
    service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
    service.update.return_value = existing_employee
    service.audit = audit_service
    return service


@pytest.fixture
def ui_form(employee_service, audit_service):
    """
    Minimal stand-in for the UI form controller / view handler.

    In a real project this would be the actual form class or page object.
    Here we keep it as a lightweight callable so tests stay framework-agnostic.
    """

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – builds the form without importing a concrete class so that the
# test module itself can be collected even before implementation exists.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_form(employee_service, audit_service):
    """Factory that lazily imports the real form class."""
    try:
        from employee_ui import EmployeeUpdateForm
        return EmployeeUpdateForm(
            employee_service=employee_service,
            audit_service=audit_service,
        )
    except ModuleNotFoundError:
        pytest.skip("employee_ui module not yet implemented")
